_meta.hardware: Snapdragon X Elite CRD (Adreno X1-85 / QNN GPU EP)
_meta.model: facebook/convnext-tiny-224 (ALL findings from this model only)
_meta.epistemics_warning: ⚠️ All findings are hypotheses derived from 1 model on 1 device. Confidence levels reflect mechanism understanding, not universal applicability. GPU EP behavior varies significantly by model architecture and Adreno driver version.

findings.title: FP32 baseline is already optimal for ConvNext on QNN GPU — no optimization pass helps
findings.observation: Full sweep of 11 passes/combinations on ConvNext QNN GPU: all returned 0% node reduction or worse latency. Baseline p50=17.7ms, p90=19.7ms, std=0.97.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: 251/0/0/0 (all ops native on GPU, zero CPU fallback). ConvNext linear layers use Reshape→MatMul→Reshape, not bare MatMul+Add — so MatMulAdd→Conv2D rewrites don't match. autoconf (gelu_fusion + matmul_add_fusion) already applied all applicable transforms.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Skip all graph optimization experiments for QNN GPU on ConvNext-class models. Use FP32 baseline directly.
findings.confidence: high — confirmed by 0% node delta on all rewrites + 251/0/0/0 analyze output
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Transformer models with bare MatMul+Add (those may benefit from rewrites)

findings.title: NHWC transformer hurts QNN GPU on Adreno X1-85 (~10% worse)
findings.observation: NHWC transformer: p50=19.5ms (+10%), p90=23.8ms (+21%), std=3.43 (3.5x worse). Consistent across multiple runs.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: Adreno X1-85 + QNN GPU EP does not benefit from explicit NHWC layout transforms. QNN GPU EP handles layout internally; forcing NHWC via ORT creates additional Reshape overhead without the memory alignment benefit.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do NOT apply nhwc-transformer for QNN GPU EP.
findings.confidence: medium — observed consistently; mechanism hypothesis, not confirmed
findings.do_not_generalize_to: Non-Adreno GPUs (NVIDIA, Intel Arc) — NHWC may help there

findings.title: winml compile appears to hurt QNN GPU (~34% regression) — SINGLE EXPERIMENT, LOW CONFIDENCE
findings.observation: FP32 + compile: p50=23.7ms vs baseline 17.7ms (+34%). Single experiment only.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN GPU EP compile (EPContext) is designed for NPU (HTP). On GPU EP, the compilation path may force a different dispatch mode that bypasses the optimized GPU shader path. QNN SDK likely has a GPU-specific compilation flow that winml compile doesn't trigger correctly.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: AVOID winml compile for QNN GPU EP. Direction (regression) is consistent with mechanism hypothesis and 34% is a large signal, but this is a single experiment. Until replicated, treat as likely harmful but not confirmed.
findings.confidence: low — single experiment. 34% gap is above DVFS noise level (CV ~0.05 → noise ~1ms, gap is 6ms). Direction probably real but magnitude uncertain.
findings.do_not_generalize_to: QNN NPU EP (compile always helps NPU)

findings.title: W8A8 QDQ hangs indefinitely on QNN GPU EP
findings.observation: Passing a W8A8 QDQ-annotated ONNX to QNN GPU EP causes infinite hang. winml build's _patch_device() sets quant=null for GPU, preventing this in normal user path.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN SDK's GPU EP does not support QDQ-quantized graphs. This is a known QNN SDK limitation. winml build already protects against this via _patch_device().
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Skip ALL quantization experiments for QNN GPU EP. Do not even attempt W8A8 or W8A16.
findings.confidence: high — hang confirmed; protection mechanism in _patch_device() confirmed by code inspection

findings.title: gelu_fusion improves latency STABILITY (p90/std) on QNN GPU, not p50
findings.observation: Raw export (287 nodes, unfused Gelu): p50=17.4ms, p90=29.2ms, std=5.90. Autoconf (251 nodes, fused Gelu): p50=17.7ms, p90=19.7ms, std=0.97. p50 nearly identical, p90 -48%, std -6x.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: 5 separate GPU kernel dispatches (Mul→Div→Erf→Mul→Add) for unfused GELU create scheduling jitter. Single Gelu kernel eliminates dispatch overhead → dramatically lower tail latency.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Always apply gelu_fusion for QNN GPU (stability benefit). Do not expect p50 improvement.
findings.confidence: high — mechanism is well-understood (GPU kernel dispatch overhead)
findings.scope: Any model with GELU activations on QNN GPU

findings.title: opset 21 on QNN GPU not yet validated
findings.observation: opset 21 sweep only run on QNN NPU. QNN GPU behavior with opset 21 is unknown.
findings.mechanism_hypothesis: QNN GPU and QNN NPU use different kernel registries. opset 21 speedup on NPU does NOT imply the same on GPU.
findings.action_for_autoconfig: Do not assume opset 21 helps QNN GPU. Run a validation experiment before adding to search space.

opset.rationale: gpu-006: opset 21 not validated for GPU. Stay at 17 until tested.
opset.dialectical_note: ⚠️ May change once opset 21 GPU experiment is run.

skip: all — QDQ hangs on GPU EP (gpu-004)
quantization.dialectical_note: ⚠️ This is a QNN SDK limitation, not winml. May change with future QNN SDK versions that support GPU quantization.

compile.dialectical_note: ⚠️ gpu-003: compile regresses QNN GPU. Confirmed by single experiment. Re-validate if winml compile behavior changes.

graph_passes.dialectical_note: ⚠️ Skip rules are ConvNext-specific. Transformer models may benefit from attention_fusion etc.
